reasoning: The baseline value reported near 2025-03-02 is 224,000 (from 2025-03-01). The FRED statistical model predicts a downward drift in the short term, with 0.36 probability of exceeding 224k for the first resolution date. Historically, initial jobless claims dip slightly in the spring (March-April) before consistently rising in early to mid-summer (June-August). For instance, summer 2024 claims routinely hit 227k-248k. I've projected lower probabilities for March and April (0.37, 0.41) due to structural seasonal lows and near-term mean reversion from the late February spike, rising to near or exactly over 0.5 for the summer dates (0.47 in May, 0.53 in August) to accommodate historical seasonality and the possibility of slow federal baseline increases from ongoing workforce reductions. Finally, the expectation regresses squarely to 0.5 for the 1-year mark due to maximal uncertainty.
